commented_code:
+---
+id: 69bba2f81c07d5d804cc4399
+title: Step 9
+challengeType: 0
+dashedName: step-9
+---
+
+# --description--
+
+In previous steps, you have created a responsive `svg` container, Now, you should draw red circle inside.
+
+```js
+const svg_circle = d3
+  .select("body")
+  .append("svg")
+  .attr("width", '800px')
+  .attr("height", '500px')
+  .style("border", '2px solid black'); 
+
+// appends a circle element at (cx,cy):(0,0) left-top position 
+svg_circle
+  .append("circle")
+  .attr("cx", 0)   // cx goes Left to Right
+  .attr("cy", 0)   // cy goes Top to Buttom
+  .attr("r", 50)   // r is radius of the circle
+  .style("fill", "red");
+```
+
+Now, you should append a `circle` element into the `svg`. If you use exactly same as above, you will see *1/4th* of a red circle, rest of the circle is outside of the circle.  
+Then you can play with different `(cx,cy)` center location to have clear understanding of co-ordinate system of `svg`.  
+
+# --hints--
+
+You should append `circle` element to the `svg`.
+
+```js
+assert.match(code, /svg\s*\.append\(\s*"circle"\s*\)/);
+```
+
+You should add center `(cx,cy)` to the circle.
+
+```js
+assert.match(code, /\.attr\(\s*"cx"\s*,\s*\d+\s*\)\s*\.attr\(\s*"cy"\s*,\s*\d+\s*\)/);
+```
+
+You should add radius `r` arrtibute to circle.
+
+```js
+assert.match(code, /\.attr\(\s*"r"\s*,\s*\d+\s*\)/);

discussion_comments.comment_body: this should allow for single or double quotes.

```suggestion
assert.match(groupByDecade.toString(), /book\.year\s*===?\s*("|')Unknown\1/);
```
This will make sure the same quotes will be used. Similar change should be applied to other occurrences.

commented_code:
+---
+id: 698673387a771e3c91468754
+title: Step 4
+challengeType: 0
+dashedName: step-4
+---
+
+# --description--
+
+The non-null assertion operator (`!`) tells the Typescript compiler that this element
+will be defined at runtime and prevent any run-time errors. 
+
+```ts
+const catPicture = document.querySelector<HTMLImageElement>('#catPicture')!;
+```
+
+# --hints--
+
+You should use the `non-null` assertion operator.
+
+```js
+const usingSelectorRegex =
+  /(const|let) cardDisplay\s*=\s*document\.querySelector<HTMLElement>.+!/;
+assert.match(code, usingSelectorRegex);
+```
+# --seed--

commented_code:
+---
+id: 694175528a794a090ea0ba74
+title: Lab Motorcycle Shop 
+challengeType: 25
+dashedName: lab-motorcycle-shop
+demoType: onClick
+---
+
+# --description--
+
+For this lab, you have been provided with all the HTML and CSS. You are going to use TypeScript to complete the functionalities of a 
+motorcycle shop page. The HTML and CSS have been provided for you. 
+
+**Objective:** Fulfill the user stories below and get all the tests to pass to complete the lab. 
+
+**User Stories:**
+
+1. You should create an `type` called `Category` that consists of the following possible values: `'Sport'`, `'Cruiser'`,`'Touring'`,`'Dirt'`, `'Adventure'`, `'Naked'`, `'Electric'` 
+2. You should create an `interface` called `Motorcycle` that has the following properties: an `id` of type `string`, an `name` of type `string`, an `manufacturer` of type `string`, a `category` of type `Category`, a `price` of type `number`, an `image_url` of type `string`, and `description` of type `string`, and a year of type `number` 
+3. You should create a function named `fetchMotorcycles` that returns a `Promise` resolving to an array of `Motorcycle` objects.
+4. You should load `Motorcycle` data from the `https://cdn.freecodecamp.org/curriculum/labs/data/motorcycles.json` endpoint. 
+5. You should create a function named `renderMotorcycleCard` that takes a single `Motorcycle` object as a parameter and returns a string containing the HTML that contains a formatted HTML string for the motorcycle card. 
+   1. The `renderMotorCycleCard` function should take a parameter called `motorcycle` of type `Motorcycle`. 
+   2. The `renderMotorCycleCard` function should return a type of `string`. 
+   3. Each MotorCycle card component should have an `img` element that has a valid image and a class of `motorcycle-card-image-container`.
+   4. Each MotorCycle card component should have an element with an class of `motorcycle-card-year-badge` that lists the year in plain text.
+   5. Each MotorCycle card component should have an element with a class of `motorcycle-card-title` that lists the name of the motorcycle in plain text.
+   6. Each MotorCycle card component should have an element with a class of `motorcycle-card-manufacturer` that lists the name of the motorcycle manufacturer in plain text.
+   7. Each MotorCycle card component should have an element with a class of `motorcycle-card-category` that lists the category of the motorcycle in plain text. 
+   8. Each MotorCycle card component should have an element with a class of `motorcycle-card-description` that lists the description of the motorcycle in plain text. 
+   9.  Each MotorCycle card component should have an element with a class of `motorcycle-card-price` that lists the price of the motorcycle in plain text. 
+   10. Each MotorCycle card component should have an element with a class of `motorcycle-card-engine` that lists the horsepower of each engine engine.
+6. You  should have a class called `MotorcycleGalleryApp`. 
+   1. The class should have an `private` array called `allMotorcycles` that is statically typed to a type of `Motorcycle`. 
+   2. That class should have a function called `renderMotorcycles` that renders the equivalent Motorcycle Card Components of the `allMotorcycles` variable inside the element with id of `motorcycle-grid`. 
+7. The `Motorcycle` elements inside of the grid can be filtered by name using an element with an id of `name-filter-input`. 
+8. The number of `Motorcycle` elements that are displayed should be listed inside of an element with an id of `results-number`.
+9.  The filter should be updated on an `input` event. 
+
+# --hints--
+
+You should have a `type` called `Category`.
+
+```js
+const catVarRegex = /type\s+Category\s*=\s*\|/

+6. You  should have a class called `MotorcycleGalleryApp`. 
+   1. The class should have an `private` array called `allMotorcycles` that is statically typed to a type of `Motorcycle`.

+7. The `Motorcycle` elements inside of the grid can be filtered by name using an element with an id of `name-filter-input`.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
This code does not pass the test
```ts
type Category = 'Sport'| 'Cruiser'|'Touring'|'Dirt'| 'Adventure'| 'Naked'| 'Electric'
```
The first `|` shouldn't be required.
```suggestion
const catVarRegex = /type\s+Category\s*=\s*\|?/
```

commented_code:
You should add a `:focus` pseudo-class to the `input` and `textarea` elements to change their border color when focused. Use a list selector in the given order.
 
 ```js
-assert.isNotEmpty(new __helpers.CSSHelp(document).getStyle('input:focus, textarea:focus').getPropertyValue('border-color'))
+assert.isNotEmpty(new __helpers.CSSHelp(document).getStyle('input:focus, textarea:focus').getPropertyValue('border-color'));
+```
+
+You should add a `:focus` pseudo-class to the `input` and `textarea` elements to remove the default outline when focused.
